title: Electric kiln slow cool cycle
notes: Programming and running a controlled slow-cool cycle after a cone-6 glaze firing, to prevent dunting (thermal shock cracking) at the cristobalite inversion point (573 degrees C), and to develop crystalline or special-effect glazes that need a hold at specific temperatures during descent. Explains the difference between free-fall, programmed hold, and crash-cool. When each approach is appropriate.
